behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the vision model’s real classification judgment (LLM-DEP). A stubbed classifier could always return "explicit" and hide real misses. Rejected System as the primary level because this behavior text is mainly about model decision correctness, not a required real external contract check.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a curated explicit-image set through the real moderation path using the real vision model, then assert each item is marked held-for-review and not published.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly model robustness/quality under adversarial text variants, which cannot be validated with deterministic stubs (LLM-DEP). Rejected Workflow because only one agentic decision stage (text classification) is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run real text-model moderation on a dataset of hate-speech obfuscation variants and assert they are consistently flagged for review.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: The key success criterion is an externally visible side effect (published vs held/dropped), requiring round-trip verification (SYSTEM TRIAD #3). Integration-only internal assertions can miss silent-drop behavior at external boundaries. Rejected Agentic because this behavior focuses on decision handoff/outcome visibility, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a clean post via the real submission API, then verify via real readback path (e.g., public feed/get endpoint) that the post is visible and not in hold state.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end latency requirement across multiple agentic steps in sequence (vision decision + text decision + final decision), matching MULTI-STEP => Workflow. Rejected Agentic because single-step agentic testing does not validate chained-step timing behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions that invoke real image and text moderation stages in sequence and measure total submission-to-final-decision latency; assert p95 <= 5 seconds.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module failure handling (moderation service + retry queue + approval logic), and can be validated by injecting an unavailable error and checking queue state (MOCK-MISS). Rejected System because the behavior text does not explicitly require validating a real external error contract shape; injected failure is sufficient for this requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service, model client adapter, and retry queue together; simulate vision-unavailable errors from the adapter and assert posts enter retry queue and are never marked approved.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency here depends on real model behavior under repeated borderline inputs (LLM-DEP); mocks would mask nondeterministic drift. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated evaluation of one agentic decision type, not a multi-stage agent pipeline handoff problem.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the target time window through the real vision moderation path and assert decision consistency rate meets threshold.
